nodes.label: AI as efficient scorer for routine tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable due to length bias
nodes.grounding_span: AI will sort out unscorable responses and/or actually do some or all of the scoring

nodes.label: AI scoring accuracy vs human scoring
nodes.grounding_span: AI often credits writing that isn't necessarily relevant to the prompt and may not credit a shorter response that gets straight to the relevant ideas

nodes.label: Human collaborative consensus in scoring
nodes.label_negative: AI-driven individual scoring without group discussion
nodes.grounding_span: we train as a group and talk as a group; sometimes coming to a consensus on how to credit or not credit something, as a group

nodes.label: Human contextual judgment for end-of-project reports
nodes.grounding_span: The reports at the end of certain projects are often tedious, but we continue to do them manually as they require examples of where scorers had difficulty and ideas that could help the director

nodes.label: AI as supplemental tool
nodes.label_negative: AI integration driven by quality improvement
nodes.grounding_span: Our corporate heads are always trying to save money, so tasks that AI can accurately perform much more quickly than humans are preferred

nodes.label: AI as augmenting human role
nodes.label_negative: AI augmenting human roles without replacement
nodes.grounding_span: I believe my responsibilities will be replaced by AI at some point

nodes.label: Efficiency and cost reduction
nodes.grounding_span: tasks that AI can accurately perform much more quickly than humans are preferred

nodes.label: Scoring accuracy and fairness
nodes.grounding_span: AI often credits writing that isn't necessarily relevant to the prompt

nodes.label: Human collaboration and team interaction
nodes.grounding_span: we train as a group and talk as a group; sometimes coming to a consensus

nodes.label: Contextual understanding and improvement
nodes.grounding_span: require examples of where scorers had difficulty and ideas that could help the director

nodes.label: Confidence in AI for routine scoring
nodes.grounding_span: It doesn't happen often, so we tend to be pretty confident with it

nodes.label: Caution about eliminating human comparison
nodes.grounding_span: We are not at the point, however, where we want to eliminate the comparison with human scoring

nodes.label: Resistance to AI for personal communication
nodes.grounding_span: the director just told me she agreed with the AI instead of me, and it was not helpful

nodes.label: Resignation to AI inevitability
nodes.grounding_span: I believe my responsibilities will be replaced by AI at some point

nodes.label: Analytical pattern-seeking in scoring discrepancies
nodes.grounding_span: Definitely analytical. I look for the patterns and am often correcting the human scorers

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of organizational constraints
nodes.grounding_span: I am able to make recommendations to my superiors, but I don't make the final decisions on AI set up
